items.title: ADO en FC Den Bosch grijpen in: komende drie jaar geen uitpubliek
items.summary: ADO Den Haag en FC Den Bosch zullen de komende drie jaar geen uitpubliek toelaten bij onderlinge wedstrijden. Dat hebben de clubs besloten naar aanleiding van de rellen die ontstonden tijdens het Eurojackpot KNVB Beker-duel op 28 oktober.

items.title: Groningen verkoopt spits Lien aan Djurgarden
items.summary: De winterse transfermarkt is geopend. In dit blog vind je alle speculaties en bevestigde transfers in Nederland. Bekijk hier ons blog voor de transferperikelen in het buitenland.

items.title: Bodø/Glimt v Manchester City: Champions League – live
items.summary: ⚽ Champions League updates; kick-off 5.45pm GMT ⚽ Live scores | Table | Follow us on Bluesky | Mail Barry City are enduring something of a crisis at the back which they have attempted to alleviate by signing Marc Guehi but like his fellow new recruit, Antoine Semenyo, the former Crystal Palace defender is ineligible to play in the Champions League until the knockout stages. Continue reading...

items.title: Interim-periode Echteld bij AZ start meteen slecht nieuws over Mijnans
items.summary: Leeroy Echteld zit woensdagavond voor het eerst als interim-trainer op de bank bij AZ. Excelsior komt naar Alkmaar om de wedstrijd, die vanwege de Conference League is verplaatst, in te halen. Echteld blikt terug op zijn eerste dagen en kijkt ook vooruit. De interim-trainer had slecht nieuws over Sven Mijnans.

items.title: Salah wint race tegen de klok: Slot heeft aanvaller terug bij CL-duel in Marseille
items.summary: Arne Slot heeft voor het Champions League-duel met Olympique Marseille weer de beschikking over Mohamed Salah, die de race tegen de klok heeft gewonnen .

items.title: VI Live: Club Brugge opent de score op eerste CL-avond 2026
items.summary: Welkom in de VI Live van dinsdag 20 januari. De Champions League gaat na een korte winterstop weer verder! We houden je hier op de hoogte van de ontwikkelingen. De VI Live van Villarreal - Ajax vind je hier .

items.title: Fury set for April comeback fight as 'top three' possible opponent is named
items.summary: Tyson Fury is holding advanced contract talks over a comeback fight in April and Arslanbek Makhmudov is among the 'top three' potential opponent, confirms his manager Spencer Brown.

items.title: Man who stalked Liverpool’s Marie Höbinger given two-year restraining order
items.summary: Mangal Dalal sent sexualised content to footballer Behaviour made Höbinger feel ‘anxious and scared’ A man who stalked the Liverpool midfielder Marie Höbinger has been handed a two-year restraining order and an 18-month community order. Westminster magistrates court heard that Mangal Dalal, 42, sent the Austria international sex messages on Instagram, told her he wanted to have babies with her and sent her pictures of underwear. Continue reading...

items.title: Fulham in talks to sign Man City winger Bobb
items.summary: Fulham are in talks to sign winger Oscar Bobb from fellow Premier League side Manchester City.

items.title: USMNT 2026 World Cup big board 4.0: Pochettino's roster coming into focus
items.summary: We're less than five months away from the USMNT's World Cup opener, and with the clock ticking down, Mauricio Pochettino's squad is coming into focus.

items.title: Ronaldo wins long-running Juve legal dispute
items.summary: Juventus have lost an appeal to overturn a court ruling that had awarded their former striker Cristiano Ronaldo millions in owed wages, the Serie A club confirmed to ESPN.

items.title: Monaco's Balogun: Playing Real Madrid 'a dream'
items.summary: United States men's national team and Monaco striker Folarin Balogun told ESPN he will achieve one of his dreams on Tuesday when the Ligue 1 club face Real Madrid at the Bernabéu in the Champions League.

items.title: Sources: Arsenal's Nwaneri set for Marseille loan
items.summary: Marseille are one of several clubs exploring a loan move for Arsenal midfielder Ethan Nwaneri, sources have told ESPN.

items.title: Source: Sporting KC majority stake sold at $700M
items.summary: A minority owner in MLS side Sporting Kansas City has completed a deal to purchase a majority stake in the club from its current owners, a source confirmed to ESPN.

items.title: Aston Villa step up pursuit of striker Abraham
items.summary: Aston Villa are stepping up their efforts to sign striker Tammy Abraham.

items.title: Arsenal’s Ethan Nwaneri poised for Marseille loan in blow to English suitors
items.summary: Crystal Palace and West Ham also keen on midfielder Oleksandr Zinchenko close to permanent Ajax move Ethan Nwaneri is poised to join Marseille on loan until the end of the season, with Arsenal keen to allow the England Under-21 midfielder to depart given he has barely featured this season. Nwaneri has played 165 minutes in the Premier League, having excelled in his breakthrough campaign, when he scored nine goals in all competitions. He has not appeared in the league since the end of November, when he came on in the north London derby. Continue reading...

items.title: Bamford: 'When you're not playing football, life feels out of sync'
items.summary: "When you’re not playing football, life feels a bit out of sync."

items.title: 'This is unheard of!' Did the Bills fire the wrong man?
items.summary: The craziness of the NFL coaching cycle continued this week with the Buffalo Bills firing head coach Sean McDermott after nine years at the helm. I must admit I was totally surprised by this one.

items.title: Huddersfield appoint Manning as head coach
items.summary: League One Huddersfield Town appoint former Norwich and Bristol City boss Liam Manning as head coach.

items.title: Why Iraola's Bournemouth future is uncertain
items.summary: With his contract expiring in the summer, Andoni Iraola's future at Bournemouth is unclear after two successful seasons in charge.
